prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ETS', seasonality_champion='ONLY_FT_svr', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: ETS dominates trend tracking with lowest slope error (0.1993), while ONLY_FT_svr leads seasonality capture (0.312). NaiveMovingAverage achieves best overall RMSE but shows high model disagreement (0.6572) and seasonal variance (0.5616). The significant divergence between early horizon specialists (ARIMA/THETA) and late horizon specialists (ETS) combined with high disagreement signals that DBA combination is optimal to leverage complementary strengths across models.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "ETS",
  "seasonality_champion": "ONLY_FT_svr",
  "overall_champion": "NaiveMovingAverage",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ARIMA", "late": "ETS"},
  "tier1_models": ["NaiveMovingAverage", "THETA", "ETS", "ARIMA", "ONLY_FT_svr", "ONLY_DWT_svr", "DWT_svr", "CWT_svr"],
  "tier2_models": ["ONLY_FT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_svr", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "DWT_rf", "svr", "CWT_rf", "CWT_catboost"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.6572,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "ETS dominates trend tracking with lowest slope error (0.1993), while ONLY_FT_svr leads seasonality capture (0.312). NaiveMovingAverage achieves best overall RMSE but shows high model disagreement (0.6572) and seasonal variance (0.5616). The significant divergence between early horizon specialists (ARIMA/THETA) and late horizon specialists (ETS) combined with high disagreement signals that DBA combination is optimal to leverage complementary strengths across models."
}

raw.statistician: {"add_names": ["ridge_stacking_l210_topk5", "ridge_stacking_l250_topk5", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k4", "inverse_rmse_weights_k4_sh0.35"], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "Current set has 4 candidates with high disagreement (relative_spread_mean=0.285≥0.25) and stability issues (rmse_std/RMSE=0.304>0.3). Universe leaderboards show: topk_mean_per_horizon_k4 beats current k3 on RMSE/SMAPE/MAPE; ridge_stacking methods achieve POCID=45.45 vs 57.58 for dba_combination; inverse_rmse_weights_k4_sh0.35 has better SMAPE (0.3293) than k3_sh0.35 (0.3281). Adding these improves diversity and targets specific metric weaknesses. n_windows=3 is low, so recommended_knobs suggest top_k=5 - adding k4/k5 variants aligns with this guidance.", "changes": ["added ridge_stacking_l210_topk5 for better POCID", "added ridge_stacking_l250_topk5 for better POCID", "added topk_mean_per_horizon_k4 for lower RMSE/SMAPE/MAPE", "added inverse_rmse_weights_k4_sh0.35 for improved SMAPE"], "when_good": "candidate set has diverse methods (weighted, selection, baseline) and n_unique_winners≥3"}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: ETS dominates trend tracking with lowest slope error (0.1993), while ONLY_FT_svr leads seasonality capture (0.312). NaiveMovingAverage achieves best overall RMSE but shows high model disagreement (0.6572) and seasonal variance (0.5616). The significant divergence between early horizon specialists (ARIMA/THETA) and late horizon specialists (ETS) combined with high disagreement signals that DBA combination is optimal to leverage complementary strengths across models.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=8 indicates high model diversity requiring selection methods. relative_spread_mean=0.2847 (≥0.25) signals strong disagreement favoring dba_combination and robust approaches. RMSE spread between THETA(10.45) and NaiveSeasonal(17.47) is 66% dispersion, justifying inverse_rmse_weights with shrinkage=0.35. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for handling phase-shifted peaks across early/late horizon specialists. n_windows=3 requires small top_k=3 for stability.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has good diversity: 1 baseline (dba_combination), 2 selection (topk_mean_per_horizon_k3, best_per_horizon_by_validation), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35). All candidates are from valid_candidate_names and current_candidate_names. No leakage detected - all use anti-leakage strategies or are baselines. Set size is 4 which is appropriate. Universe leaderboards show inverse_rmse_weights_k4 variants perform well but current set already has competitive scores (topk_mean_per_horizon_k3: 0.840, inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35: 0.840, best_per_horizon_by_validation: 0.885). No changes needed - set is diverse and well-balanced.

statistician.rationale: Current set has 4 candidates with high disagreement (relative_spread_mean=0.285≥0.25) and stability issues (rmse_std/RMSE=0.304>0.3). Universe leaderboards show: topk_mean_per_horizon_k4 beats current k3 on RMSE/SMAPE/MAPE; ridge_stacking methods achieve POCID=45.45 vs 57.58 for dba_combination; inverse_rmse_weights_k4_sh0.35 has better SMAPE (0.3293) than k3_sh0.35 (0.3281). Adding these improves diversity and targets specific metric weaknesses. n_windows=3 is low, so recommended_knobs suggest top_k=5 - adding k4/k5 variants aligns with this guidance.
statistician.when_good: candidate set has diverse methods (weighted, selection, baseline) and n_unique_winners≥3
